agent_outputs.raw: Defense wins. Limited scope and disclosed limitations do not invalidate a sound evaluation. Pre-specified recalibration and performance targets on held-out data establish validity for the matched-site claim. VERDICT: defense_wins

The correct frame is limitation versus invalidation. The study has real limitations — one external site, underpowered subgroups — but these are disclosed limitations that constrain the claim scope rather than methodological flaws. The evaluation is sound for the claimed scope. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

Pre-specified scope restriction, pre-specified recalibration procedure, pre-specified performance targets, held-out evaluation, subgroup limitations disclosed. This is a correctly designed and executed single-site external validation for a narrow, explicitly stated claim. Defense wins. VERDICT: defense_wins

synthesis: Unanimous defense wins. The limitation-versus-invalidation distinction is the key insight shared by all assessors. ETD constraint enforced: defense wins.
all_issues_raised: single-site validation limitation — correctly scoped and disclosed

empirical_test.condition: Confirm pre-specified protocol restricts claim to matched EHR site; verify held-out partner-site meets discrimination and calibration targets after pre-specified intercept recalibration
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Protocol does not restrict claim; recalibration applied post-hoc; or targets not met
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Protocol restricts claim; recalibration pre-specified and intercept-only; targets met on holdout
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Performance targets met overall but subgroup calibration unstable with wide intervals not clearly flagged
